nativeRange.habitat: Shaded, protected recesses in rocky reefs, boulder-strewn slopes, and along walls, typically at depths of about 3–21 m

diet.notes: Generally advised to offer a varied diet of meaty frozen foods such as mysis shrimp, krill, squid, brine shrimp, and chopped clam, fed once or twice daily. Hard-shelled prey items (clams on the half shell, small snails, whole shrimp in shell) are commonly recommended to help wear down the continuously growing beak-like fused teeth. Has been reported to graze on filamentous algae and small benthic invertebrates as well.

summary: A small Eastern Pacific sharpnose puffer reaching roughly 8–10 cm, with a dark brown body covered in fine white spots. Generally peaceful but intolerant of conspecifics and best kept singly with reef-safe caution.
careNotes:
The Central American Sharpnose Puffer is endemic to the Eastern Central Pacific, ranging from Guaymas (Mexico) south to Panama and across to the Galapagos Islands. It tends to inhabit shaded recesses on rocky reefs, boulder slopes, and walls between roughly 3 and 21 m depth.

This species is generally considered to have a peaceful disposition toward unrelated tankmates but is notably intolerant of other Canthigaster pufferfish; it is commonly recommended that only one be kept per aquarium unless a confirmed mated pair is acquired. FishBase describes the species as monogamous in the wild.

Like other sharpnose puffers, it is generally considered reef-safe only with caution: it has been reported to nip at corals, tubeworms, and ornamental invertebrates, and is likely to consume small crustaceans that form part of a reef cleanup crew. A fish-only-with-live-rock (FOWLR) setup is commonly preferred.

Its beak-like fused teeth grow continuously; without hard-shelled prey items, the teeth may overgrow. Offering clams on the half shell, small snails, or whole shrimp on a regular basis is commonly recommended to keep the teeth worn down.

Like other members of the family Tetraodontidae, this species can release potent toxins (tetrodotoxin and related compounds) when severely stressed or upon death, which has been reported to cause tank-wide mortality events. Handling should be done with a container rather than a net, as netting can provoke inflation and stress.

Cross-source notes: Tank-size recommendations spanned 40 gal (~150 L, Saltwaterfish.com), 50 gal (~190 L, LiveAquaria), and 55 gal (~208 L, Aquatics Unlimited and Reefs.com). A final value of 200 L was selected as a cautious round number toward the upper end of the cluster, consistent with the species' moderate activity and need for crevice-rich rockwork. Maximum size was averaged across LiveAquaria/Aquatics Unlimited/Reefs.com (~3.5 in ≈ 8.9 cm), Saltwaterfish.com (4 in ≈ 10.2 cm), and FishBase (9.0 cm TL), yielding a working range of 8–10 cm. Temperature was averaged across aquarium hobby sources (22.2–25.6°C, i.e. 72–78°F) and FishBase wild-range data (24.2–29.2°C), yielding a practical aquarium range of approximately 23–27°C. Care level disagreed between LiveAquaria (Moderate) and Aquatics Unlimited/Saltwaterfish.com (Easy); 'intermediate' was selected as the middle value, reflecting the dental-maintenance and reef-caution requirements. Temperament was 'peaceful' on three sources vs. 'semi-aggressive' on one (Saltwaterfish.com); 'peaceful' was selected as the majority answer, with conspecific intolerance documented separately.

primary.name: LiveAquaria
primary.notes: Primary husbandry source. Provided tank size (50 gal), care level (Moderate), temperament (Peaceful), reef compatibility (With Caution), max size (3.5 in), water parameters (72–78°F, pH 8.1–8.4, dKH 8–12, SG 1.020–1.025), and origin (Central America).

additional.name: FishBase
additional.notes: Cross-checked taxonomy (Tetraodontidae, Tetraodontiformes), max size (9.0 cm TL), native range (Eastern Central Pacific: Guaymas, Mexico to Panama and the Galapagos Islands), depth (3–21 m), wild temperature range (24.2–29.2°C), monogamous mating system, and IUCN Least Concern status.

additional.name: Aquatics Unlimited
additional.notes: Confirmed minimum tank size (55 gal / ~208 L), peaceful temperament, easy/moderate care level, max size (~3.5 in), and conspecific intolerance ('intolerant of each other; keep only one per aquarium').

additional.name: Saltwaterfish.com
additional.notes: Outlier source. Listed minimum tank size as 40 gal, temperament as semi-aggressive, max size as 4 in, and described diet as omnivorous (consumes small crustaceans). Used as additional cross-reference for max-size averaging; tank size considered conservative and not adopted.

additional.name: Reefs.com
additional.notes: Returned 403 on direct WebFetch; care summary inferred from search-engine snippet (55 gal / 200 L recommended, peaceful, conspecific intolerance).
